Overview

Address
671 ALEXIAN WAY, SIGNAL MOUNTAIN, TN 37377
Phone

Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.20 3.69 4.40 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.69 0.65 0.72 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.32 0.82 1.38 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.19 2.22 2.29 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.01 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.95
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.44
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.13
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
56.6%
annual
RN turnover
64.7%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-12-10 8 8 1 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-09-25 19 3 16 376 1 376
Total weighted health score
130.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (28)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

22 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
5 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-12-10 2025-12-29
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. G 2025-12-10 2025-11-17
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-12
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-12
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-08-07 2024-09-01
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-08-07 2024-09-01
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-08-07 2024-09-01
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2023-12-13 2024-01-12
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-12-13 2024-01-12
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2023-12-13 2024-01-12
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. E 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. J 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. J 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. J 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. F 2023-10-11 2023-11-21
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2019-11-20 2019-12-20
